MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-03-25 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JUNE-03-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AGAINST THE CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION. PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS' LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission To Plaintiff Deborah Cook, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID SCHOOP'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Deem Requests For Admission To Plaintiff Deborah Cook, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant David Schoop's motion to deem admitted set one requests for admission and for monetary sanctions is granted in large part. Schoop's set one requests for admission are deemed admitted due to plaintiff Deborah Cook's failure to serve responses to that discovery. Cook must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $510 no later than May 12, 2026. No opposition filed.

A proposed order has been submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action for general negligence against the Chinese Hospital Association and the second cause of action against the Chinese Hospital Association. Plaintiff has 20 days' leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
